text: Planning a trip can be almost as exciting as the journey itself. I spent weeks researching my destination, booking flights, and creating a loose itinerary. When I finally arrived, everything felt different. The new culture was fascinating, from the way people greeted each other to the rhythm of daily life. Tasting local food was a highlight; I tried dishes I couldn't even name. Language barriers were a challenge, but I learned to communicate with gestures and a few basic phrases. These small difficulties became part of the adventure. Now, looking back, the best memories are not just the sights, but the unexpected moments and the people I met along the way.
